rationale: Keep: the cited paper appears plausibly substantive as a theory/mechanism source, since it develops and experimentally tests social comparison/loss-aversion effects in marketing incentive contests, while the citing paper explicitly builds a behavioral model with social preferences and studies social-tie effects in sales incentives. Given this strong construct and domain overlap (both incentive-aligned lab studies of effort under socially mediated compensation), this is not clearly an incidental background mention.
